codeReview.summary: The standalone proxy server is correctly implemented. It decouples the proxy from OpenCode plugin architecture by spawning cursor-agent as a subprocess directly, without requiring OpenCode to be running. All three expected behaviors are satisfied: (1) proxy can start via 'bun run proxy' or 'node dist/proxy/standalone-server.js', (2) proxy does not require OpenCode, (3) no OpenCode plugin hooks are used.

issues.description: Minor discrepancy: The handoff verification states health endpoint returns version '1.0.0', but the code at line 472 returns version '2.3.20'. The package.json shows version 2.3.20, so the code is correct - the handoff observation appears to be from an earlier version of the code or a stale observation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows existing patterns in the codebase. The worker added a new entry point (standalone-server.ts) and updated package.json with scripts and bin entries, which aligns with how other CLI entry points (open-cursor, cursor-discover, mcptool) are structured.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json bin entry 'cursor-proxy': 'dist/proxy/standalone-server.js' follows the same pattern as other binaries

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used the proxy service as documented in services.yaml (bun run proxy on port 32124). No undocumented services were started.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows proxy started and tested on port 32124, matching services.yaml configuration

summary: The feature implementation is correct and complete. A standalone proxy server entry point was created at src/proxy/standalone-server.ts that can run independently via 'bun run proxy' or 'node dist/proxy/standalone-server.js'. The server uses cursor-agent directly without any OpenCode plugin dependencies. All expected behaviors are satisfied. One minor non-blocking issue: a version string discrepancy between the handoff observation and actual code.
